g.text: * This cuts power for the TPD chip when USB-C is not

g.desc: * This cuts power for the TPD chip when USB-C is not

g.text: connected. The TPD*S300A needs to be powered together

g.desc: connected. The TPD*S300A needs to be powered together

g.text: with the MCU (the USB-PD controller), hence why an

g.desc: with the MCU (the USB-PD controller), hence why an

g.text: external power is necessary, and why it's the same

g.desc: external power is necessary, and why it's the same

g.text: * The board supports Source and Sink roles, including

g.desc: * The board supports Source and Sink roles, including

g.text: DRP. The TPD will initially be powered, but

g.desc: DRP. The TPD will initially be powered, but

g.text: considering its rather high quiescent, it is a good

g.desc: considering its rather high quiescent, it is a good

g.text: idea to turn off the TPD whenever the USB-PD role

g.desc: idea to turn off the TPD whenever the USB-PD role

g.text: or MCU's mode of operation allows for it.

g.desc: or MCU's mode of operation allows for it.

g.text: [input-vbus] USB-C Connector: Protections, Filter and VUSB Discharge

g.desc: [input-vbus] USB-C Connector: Protections, Filter and VUSB Discharge

g.text: * No proper calculation for this Pi-like filter... But perhaps it matters not.

g.desc: * No proper calculation for this Pi-like filter... But perhaps it matters not.

g.text: * ESD is bidirectional because it's what I have in stock, but it can be

g.desc: * ESD is bidirectional because it's what I have in stock, but it can be

g.text: replaced with a unidirectional one with the same package.

g.desc: replaced with a unidirectional one with the same package.
